Event: Claims she must be pregnant because a stork is nesting nearby
Explanation: Her delusion causes confusion and potential anxiety for herself with no benefit, while not clearly affecting others.

Event: Attempts scissoring over Skype
Explanation: She seeks sexual gratification but risks embarrassment/relationship issues without clear gain for the partner.

Event: Dates Kurt as his “beard” to help him appear straight
Explanation: She gains companionship and social status while helping Kurt’s closeting goals at the time.

Event: Reacts graciously to being called stupid but quietly holds a grudge
Explanation: Polite response preserves her image while she later withholds goodwill from them.

Event: Runs for or accepts being framed as Betty in love triangle with Puck and Santana
Explanation: She pursues romantic attention which complicates others’ relationships.

Event: Becomes Veronica to Tina’s Betty in triangle with Artie
Explanation: She seeks Artie’s attention at Tina’s expense, creating tension.

Event: Serves as Archie in triangle with Artie and Santana
Explanation: She benefits from dual romantic interest while fostering rivalry.

Event: Claims she lost virginity to “alien” visiting her tent
Explanation: If taken as bed trick, she was deceived and sexually exploited, harming herself with unclear effects on others.

Event: Correctly reads others’ emotions and befriends people easily
Explanation: Her social savvy helps her build networks and gives others emotional understanding.

Event: Answers “a male duck” when asked what a ballad is
Explanation: Wrong academically but lightens mood and harms no one; maintains her likable persona.

Event: Teaches “Single Ladies” choreography as professional dancer
Explanation: She showcases her skill and boosts group performance quality.

Event: Returns for 100th episode and accepts Santana’s offer to go to New York
Explanation: She rekindles a key relationship and opens new opportunities for both.

Event: Makes out frequently with boys while show delays kissing girlfriend
Explanation: She gains experience/status while reinforcing unequal visibility for her same-sex relationship.

Event: Stops learning alphabet after mixing up M and N
Explanation: She harms her own education without affecting others significantly.

Event: Frames herself as living in Britney Spears’s shadow
Explanation: Her insecurity limits self-esteem with no benefit to her or others.

Event: Runs a popular internet vlog/talk show
Explanation: She gains attention/creative outlet while entertaining viewers.

Event: Remains ditzy/sweet yet sticks with Santana, an “Alpha Bitch”
Explanation: She maintains status and emotional support while softening Santana somewhat.

Event: Raises left hand when told to raise right hand
Explanation: Minor confusion but mostly comic, providing levity without real loss.

Event: Tells Coach Sylvester she sometimes forgets her middle name
Explanation: Reveals vulnerability, prompting humor but not real harm.

Event: Claims Kurt shouldn’t run for class president because he’s male
Explanation: She undermines his chances based on prejudice while boosting her preferred outcome.

Event: Wishes at Mall Santa for Artie to be able to walk
Explanation: Sincere wish costs her nothing and expresses deep care for Artie.

Event: Becomes markedly more ditzy after Santana dumps her
Explanation: Emotional collapse harms her functioning with little effect on others.

Event: Quickly befriends almost anyone due to being “too stupid to be mean”
Explanation: Her nonjudgmental attitude benefits her network and makes others feel accepted.

Event: Leaves New Directions for Troubletones
Explanation: She pursues a seemingly better opportunity, weakening her old group.

Event: Is bribed with Dots to join the Brainiacs
Explanation: She gains candy and participation while helping the team succeed.

Event: Talks to herself believing she’s doing a voiceover
Explanation: Her confusion mildly undermines her credibility but amuses others without harm.

Event: Wears friendship bracelet with Santana
Explanation: Physical token reinforces emotional bond for both.

Event: Makes mathematical breakthrough via “Brittany Code” and gets MIT offer
Explanation: She gains a life-changing scholarship and advances mathematics.

Event: Reveals jealousy of Britney Spears because of her name
Explanation: Her fixation reduces her self-worth with no clear upside.

Event: Plots a sham election to be Class President for life
Explanation: She seeks lasting power at the expense of democratic fairness.

Event: Gets caught up in Lord Tubbington’s Ponzi scheme
Explanation: She loses out to a fraud while feeding into a harmful scheme.

Event: Secretly tapes Glee rehearsals for Sue
Explanation: She aims to gain favor with Sue by betraying her club, risking their performance security.

Event: Admits to Mr. Schue “Coach Sylvester didn’t tell me to do this” when caught filming
Explanation: She sacrifices her usefulness to Sue and exposes the scheme, lightly protecting the club.

Event: Doesn’t brush teeth and washes mouth with soda
Explanation: Severely harms her own health without helping anyone.

Event: Is part of Quinn’s girl posse engaging in low-level meanness
Explanation: She enjoys social status and security while contributing to others’ social harm.

Event: Dates Artie initially to make Santana jealous
Explanation: She manipulates Artie’s feelings to influence Santana with little benefit to him.

Event: Secretly tapes Glee rehearsals as The Mole for Sue
Explanation: She trades group trust and possibly performance strategy for Sue’s approval.

Event: Joins brainiacs team and contributes to their success
Explanation: Her presence improves her academic profile and benefits the team.

Event: Breaks down after Santana leaves: junk food, violence, shaving head attempt, lip-synching
Explanation: She deliberately hits rock bottom, causing self-harm for a possible future comeback.

Event: Gets involved in Lord Tubbington’s Ponzi scheme
Explanation: She loses resources and dignity, indirectly fueling a scam.

Event: Tries to do vocal runs poorly in “Duets”
Explanation: She pushes beyond her ability, slightly harming performance quality for personal spotlight.

Event: Refuses to accept Kurt romantically due to incompatible orientation
Explanation: She avoids a doomed relationship, sparing both from long-term hurt.

Event: Tries to secretly tape club rehearsals for Sue
Explanation: She again betrays the group for Sue’s benefit with little gain.

Event: Doesn’t brush teeth and instead uses soda
Explanation: Redundant but reiterated self-harmful habit.

Event: Takes all her antibiotics at once, causing temporary brain damage/memory loss
Explanation: Severe self-harm with no benefit and negligible impact on others.

Event: Goes skiing without skis
Explanation: She endangers herself through incompetence, affecting no one else.

Event: Says she doesn’t know how to turn on a computer
Explanation: Her lack of basic skills limits her opportunities but amuses others.

Event: Rips off shirt while drunk and dances in bra for Artie
Explanation: She seeks attention/pleasure while risking self-respect and creating awkwardness.

Event: Cheats on Artie around time of Mike suspicion
Explanation: She gets additional romantic/sexual gratification while hurting Artie.

Event: Makes out with nearly everyone in school
Explanation: She gains experience and popularity but increases emotional/health risks for others.

Event: Serves as ineffective mole for Sue, failing to truly hurt Glee club
Explanation: She risks her own trustworthiness yet accidentally protects the club.

Event: Quits Cheerios and lets her hair down
Explanation: She abandons a restrictive environment, improving her autonomy and still doing fine socially.

Event: Gets drunk and abandons shirt at Rachel’s house
Explanation: She enjoys the party but loses clothing and dignity.

Event: Runs for and becomes Student Council President
Explanation: She gains leadership experience while presumably representing students.

Event: Leaves high school early to enroll at MIT
Explanation: Major educational and career leap with no apparent harm to others.

Event: Makes out with many people, then realizes multiplied STI/social risk
Explanation: Her past choices increase her own risk and anxiety without much benefit to others.

Event: Decides she is better than Britney Spears and demands every solo
Explanation: She chases spotlight at the cost of group equity and morale.

Event: Asks Artie what he asked from Santa, sincerely believing in Santa
Explanation: Her childlike belief deepens emotional intimacy and causes no harm.

Event: Gives herself a dollhouse and Cindy Lou Who styling at Christmas
Explanation: Her playful rituals provide self-comfort and harmless charm.

Event: Becomes trendsetter whose style is mass copied
Explanation: She gains status and others enjoy participating in a positive trend.

Event: Rachel tries to leverage Brittany’s trendiness, which backfires
Explanation: Rachel’s manipulative attempt fails and hurts her own aims while not harming Brittany.
